channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russian paramilitary soldiers killed in friendly fire attack by North Koreans after enlisting DPRK help
item.description: North Korean troops recently killed Russian soldiers in a friendly fire attack, according to Ukrainian intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Filipino woman who was on death row in Indonesia will return home after almost 15 years in prison
item.description: A Filipino woman who was on death row in Indonesia — and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015 — was moved late Sunday to a female prison in Indonesia’s capital, from where she will be flown back to her home country.
item.dc:creator: Slamet Riyadi And Niniek Karmini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bolivia's divisive popular vote for senior judges offers lessons for Mexico
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Campaigning in Bolivia for Sunday's judicial election may be strictly forbidden, but look closer on the streets of the capital La Paz and you’ll find that some candidates have sneakily plastered their faces on packs of corn puffs and others have slipped subtle slogans into off
item.dc:creator: PAOLA FLORES and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump once stopped Erdogan attacks on US-backed Kurds in Syria, and has opportunity to do it again: Jack Keane
item.description: As the conflict expands between pro-Turkey Islamist forces and the pro-American Kurdish forces, there are growing efforts to rope in Ankara’s offensive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oil spill in Kerch Strait after two Russian oil tankers seriously damaged in storm
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Two Russian oil tankers have been seriously damaged following a storm in the Kerch Strait, spilling oil and sparking an emergency rescue operation, Russian officials told state news outlets Sunday. Some reports said at least one of the vessels later sank.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian President Lula discharged from hospital after surgery to stop brain bleed
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva was discharged from the hospital Sunday after undergoing surgery to stop a brain bleed.
item.dc:creator: THIAGO MOSTAZO and MAYCRON ABADE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oil spill in Kerch Strait after two Russian oil tankers seriously damaged in storm
item.description: At least one person has died after two Russian oil tankers took heavy storm damage in the Kerch Strait, spilling oil and sparking an emergency rescue operation, Russian officials told state news outlets.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Isak Andic, founder of Spanish fashion brand Mango, dies in accident, aged 71
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Isak Andic, the founder of Spanish fashion brand Mango, has died after suffering an accident, the company said on Saturday. He was 71.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s president impeached over brief martial law decree
item.description: South Korea's parliament voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol early Saturday morning, after a brief decree of martial law thrust the country into temporary political peril. The motion to impeach Yoon passed with a 204-85 vote, The Associated Press (AP) reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey seeks to purge pro-US Kurdish force that helped defeat Islamic State in Syria
item.description: A Turkish strongman exploits chaos in Syria to oust pro-American Syrian Kurds from border area while solidifying expansion in war-torn nation
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military flies missing American out of Syria
item.description: The U.S. military on Friday flew a missing American citizen out of Syria this week, after the stunning fall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad's regime. Travis Timmerman was transferred to U.S. forces in Syria on Friday, according to a defense official, and was flown to Jordan. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russia batters Ukraine power grid amid rising concern Putin could order ballistic missile attack this weekend
item.description: Ukraine's energy infrastructure was again pummeled in a "massive blow" by Russian missiles and drones on Friday, just days after the U.S. warned Russia might be planning another Oreshnik missile strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hearings are done: Now the wait begins for verdict on closely-watched climate case at UN’s top court
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A lawyer for a small South Pacific island nation told the world’s highest court to heed her warning.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU nations agree a crackdown on migrant smugglers. Some fear it may target people trying to help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union countries agreed on Friday a draft law aimed at preventing and countering migrant smuggling, which critics say could be used to target people or charity groups that try to help migrants in trouble.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia targets Ukrainian infrastructure with a massive attack by cruise missiles and drones
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a massive aerial attack against Ukraine on Friday, firing 93 cruise and ballistic missiles and almost 200 drones, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, describing it as one of the heaviest bombardments of the country's energy sector since Russia's full-sc
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew barred from UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the U.K. because of concerns he poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Travis Timmerman, the missing American apparently found in Syria
item.description: An American man found wandering the streets outside the Syrian capital of Damascus barefoot identified himself as Pete Travis Timmerman on Thursday. Timmerman was last seen in Budapest, Hungary, in June and had not been heard from in months.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies travel bans on Georgia officials for undermining October election
item.description: President Biden is readying visa restrictions on officials in the country of Georgia amid a government crackdown on mass protests against the claimed victory by the Georgian Dream party, which critics say is turning the country toward Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Syria's liberated political prisons reveal grim reality of Bashar Assad's regime of torture
item.description: The fall of Bashar Assad's regime in Syria has provided a unobstructed view of the torture and persecution inflicted on the nation's prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's Justice Ministry imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Justice Ministry on Monday imposed an overseas travel ban on President Yoon Suk Yeol as authorities investigate allegations of rebellion and other charges in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
